item.title: 100% relatable
item.description: Went to the HVW office today to sign for my payment, thinking it’d be quick — but turns out there’s a processing delay, so I won’t get it until next week. Apparently, I’m not the only one. Nothing to do but check my bank account next Friday. Just my luck. Not ideal, but at least they didn’t lose my paperwork or send me on a wild goose chase, so there’s that. I'm so used to constant disappointments that I just gotta laugh at this. Meanwhile, the job hunt continues! A friendly girl at a print shop helped me make some copies of my CV, so I’ve been out applying everywhere I can, all week. 22 applications so far. 5 call-backs for next week. If you feel that you’re able to help me get through the next few days, check my bio or DM me ❤️ (Bonus: you’ll get a dad joke or a bad pun!) Be excellent to each other x
item.dc:creator: Skeebeedeebop

item.title: What Happened Today #55
item.description: The White House still denies leaking military strike plans while still finding time to slash child vaccine programs and send plainclothes and masked police to arrest foreign students. This is a power play with threats disguised as policy. Read, share, subscribe free at https://govbrief.today #GovBriefToday #Resist 1. https://www.scrippsnews.com/politics/president-trumps-first-100-days/full-transcript-of-trump-administrations-yemen-attack-plans-released-by-the-atlantic 2. https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/cly341xr45vo 3. https://www.cnn.com/2025/03/26/politics/beryl-howell-perkins-coie-trump/index.html 4. https://www.npr.org/2025/03/26/nx-s1-5341780/social-security-administration-identity-requirements 5. https://thehill.com/regulation/court-battles/5215883-appeals-court-boasberg-alien-enemies-act/ 6. https://www.axios.com/2025/03/27/el-salvador-noem-venezuelans-cecot-prison-immigration 7. https://www.politico.com/news/2025/03/26/noem-fema-restructure-eliminate-grants-00250610 8. https://www.usatoday.com/story/news/politics/2025/03/26/trump-supreme-court-teacher-training-grants/82573690007/ 9. https://www.theguardian.com/us-news/2025/mar/26/npr-pbs-mtg-hearing-doge 10. https://www.reuters.com/business/healthcare-pharmaceuticals/trump-administration-end-funding-child-vaccines-developing-countries-new-york-2025-03-26/ 11. https://www.nbcnews.com/news/us-news/federal-immigration-authorities-detain-international-tufts-graduate-st-rcna198158 12. https://www.wbur.org/news/2025/03/26/protesters-demand-tufts-students-release-following-arrest-by-immigration-officials 13. https://www.nytimes.com/2025/03/26/us/politics/judge-pause-firings.html 14. https://www.msn.com/en-ph/news/us/us-prosecutors-probe-tip-about-timing-of-pfizer-s-covid-vaccine-wsj-reports/ar-AA1BIRsB 15. https://www.wired.com/story/michael-waltz-left-his-venmo-public/ 16. https://www.usatoday.com/story/news/nation/2025/03/27/ohio-measles-outbreak-kansas-officials/82684343007/ 17. https://www.yahoo.com/news/european-union-urges-citizens-stockpile-131434807.html 18. https://www.barrons.com/articles/trump-crypto-stablecoin-chaos-3ff648bb 19. https://docs.google.com/document/d/1pDZmq1ir52LfzNR2TNkT8Vs5x_Hh4xAqJ-wBkos-Heg/edit?tab=t.0

item.title: "Never shall I forget that night,the first night in camp, which has turned my life into one long night, seven times cursed and seven times sealed. Never shall I forget that smoke. Never shall I forget the little faces of the children, whose bodies I saw
item.description: "Never shall I forget that night, the first night in camp, which has turned my life into one long night, seven times cursed and seven times sealed. Never shall I forget that smoke. Never shall I forget the little faces of the children, whose bodies I saw turned into wreaths of smoke beneath a silent blue sky." Elie Wiesel - Night
item.dc:creator: Joyika

item.dc:creator: LitterboxComics

item.title: Zürich Switzerland - An AlsoAshley Travel Blog
item.description:
I made my way over to Zürich Switzerland last month - and it was such a blast! AND I finally made one of those travel videos for this trip that instagram always tries to shove at you. It was actually pretty difficult to remind myself to snap a couple seconds of video wherever I went - I'm much more a person who likes to experience everything outside of the camera the moment I'm not shooting cosplay lol But, I was happy I did, and it also means I get to share a bit of the experience with all you, so even better!
First off, Switzerland in general was so welcoming, and it felt great just being me there the entire time. For those of you curious about my day to day life in the states, yes, I will still sometimes fave to go in "guy mode" depending on where (and what time of day) I go places... For example, thanks to certain new executive orders - how I present in an airport with my ID and passport... It's just the reality of where we are as a country. For me, this is one of my big draws to travel, as everywhere I have gone - nobody cares. But in a good way. And it can be tough having to change who I am to get back through the airport... All that being said, the tough stuff is out of the way, now let's see some gorgeous scenery!!
This is the view of the city from Lindenhof, a popular tourist destination that has a little park up on a hill to allow for a lovely view! You can see my hotel from here, right up that alley on the right - the yellow building. All of Zürich was walkable, and I can't tell you how amazing that feels. Weather was gorgeous for end of February as well! A good time to go to avoid tourist season.
View of the Limmat river from Rudolf-Brun-Brücke! Why the Zürich sky felt more blue than anywhere I have been is beyond me :D
Fun Fact about me - I speak German. According to everybody in Zürich, I also speak it quite well! It was actually so much fun getting my vocabulary back in just a few days and just chatting with folks... or ordering silly drinks to get silly under beautiful clocktowers...
My favorite part of visiting anywhere in Europe is just the walking. I spend hours a day just taking in everything and finding little nooks and crannies rather than alcoves (In Bruges reference) to explore and take pics of. The architecture is just so different.
The only reason I walk so much in Europe is to make up for how much I eat... Bauern Schänke was one of the fanciest places I have ever eaten. I am a massive foodie, and can't recommend this place enough.
It is also the first place I have ever eaten caviar at... I don't understand movies and TV acting like this is weird food, or gross food as a joke for decades... It's amazing.
Something about this corner just sang to me. That staircase to the right leads up to Lindenhof. I sat here for about 30 minutes and just let the world walk by.
The Limmat as the sun set... I walked the river every day.
Going to another fancy restaurant so just used it as an excuse to get dressy.
On the lest Spätzle with truffles shaved on top right at the table. This is the equivalent of the greatest version of mac and cheese you can imagine :P And on the right - Sour Cream Ice cream. Yup, I didn't know that was a thing, but I am glad it is!
Okay so... You saw it in the video... Yes, I went to a James Blunt concert and got cute for it... When I told people that, they all go "Who?" and then I sing the first lines of "You're Beautiful" and everybody goes - "Oh! That guy!" And I then explain he has so many better songs - and even he makes a song in every album making fun of = "You're Beautiful"... Look, is the main reason I went to Switzerland so I could go to a James Blunt concert? Yes... is that insane to most people? Also yes... Also look, those seats filled up behind me at the concert OKAY. There are dozens of real James Blunt, DOZENS.
Luzern! I took a gorgeous train ride about 50 minutes out of the city, to experience Luzern. Such a gorgeous little tourist destination that literal had a castle hovering above you where ever you walked downtown. This was probably my favorite part of the trip, and I wish I had spent more than one day there, but maybe my short time there is what made it even more special.
View of the tower of Kapellbrücke - a beautiful bridge spanning the river downtown. And a glorious view of the baked gelato I got... Raspberry and chocolate wrapped up and backed in a pastry... At this point I knew I didn't want to leave :P
View down the Reuss... Also, looking up and seeing an ancient castle wall barricading and encircling the city is such a surreal feeling... Yes it made me feel like a princess...
The face you make when someone reminds you that you are leaving Switzerland in about 12 hours... Well everybody, thanks for taking this ride with me! I hope you enjoyed this, and I hope you might also use this post as inspiration to make your own way over to Zürich and Switzerland! Most of the places I visit are from blogs like this that I see, so I hope it makes you wanna go too! And don't worry, you don't have to know German to enjoy Switzerland, everybody there is friendly and speaks immaculate English :P Tschüss!!
item.dc:creator: Alsoashley

item.title: Who the F is still buying HP?
item.description:
HP printer class action lawsuit ends in disappointment Only a few recent models will offer the option to turn down "security updates" that lock you into HP ink. At this point if you're still buying printers from hewlett-packard, I don't even have any sympathy for you. We've known about this for decades. They make money on overpriced ink, so they will do anything they can to keep you buying HP ink.
People are pretty pissed off at HP printers. Wait, hang on a sec, let me revise that statement: People are pretty pissed off at all printers, perpetually, but HP printers in particular have been extremely irksome for the last few years. A class-action lawsuit was filed over HP’s attempts to lock users into its expensive first-party ink, but the results are probably far short of what you want to hear. Last week, a California federal judge finally resolved a lawsuit that stretched over more than four years, ending in HP neither admitting fault nor compensating the vast majority of customers affected by software updates that were called “malware” by the litigants. Just three parties that brought the suit will get $5,000 USD each, and customers will have the option to roll back the offending updates on 21 models of LaserJet printer.
item.dc:creator: nobodyspecial995
